messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Adaptation Genomics course (online)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 We are thrilled to announce our upcoming online course, "Adaptation Genomics" taking place from June 24th to June 28th, 2024. There are still a few seats available!

 Course Website: [  https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/courseadaptationgenomics/ ]( https://nam10.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.physalia-courses.org%2Fcourses-workshops%2Fcourseadaptationgenomics%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cagdg-list%40lists.colostate.edu%7Cac70c991c31a48f803e708dc84a19c51%7Cafb58802ff7a4bb1ab21367ff2ecfc8b%7C0%7C0%7C638531076840007464%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=pdlMd3bvob0u6REZq9uXx4odlS9MhLeoQBk7s9B4hWA%3D&reserved=0 ) 

 This course introduces the genomic basis of adaptation through population genomics approaches. Participants will learn to handle raw genomic data, detect candidate genomic regions involved in local adaptation, and perform hands-on bioinformatics exercises.

 Key skills covered include:

 - Handling genomic data
 - Calculating population genetic statistics
 - Visualizing genetic population structure
 - Detecting adaptive divergence and local adaptation regions
 - Identifying signatures of selection
 - Detecting structural variants of adaptive significance

 For a full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [  https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://nam10.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.physalia-courses.org%2Fcourses-workshops%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cagdg-list%40lists.colostate.edu%7Cac70c991c31a48f803e708dc84a19c51%7Cafb58802ff7a4bb1ab21367ff2ecfc8b%7C0%7C0%7C638531076840007464%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=QCujr29eVgEscxm3wXFHBoWFd3tJnOcN3zq8lmmsfZI%3D&reserved=0 ) .

 Best regards,

 Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D  
 Physalia-courses Director  
 info at physalia-courses.org  
 Mobile: +49 17645230846
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] ONLINE COURSE – Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR06)
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSE – Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR06)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R
With The VEGAN package (VGNR06)

https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr06/

18-22 September 2024

Please feel free to share!

This 5-day course will cover R concepts, methods, and tools that can be
used to analyze community ecology data. The course will review data
processing techniques relevant to multivariate data sets. We will cover
diversity indices, distance measures and distance-based multivariate
methods, clustering, classification and ordination techniques using the R
package VEGAN. We will use real-world empirical data sets to motivate
analyses, such as describing patterns along gradients of environ-mental or
anthropogenic disturbances, quantifying the effects of continuous and
discrete predictors. We will emphasise visualisation and reproducible
workflows as well as good programming practices. The modules will consist
of introductory lectures, guided computer coding, and participant
exercises. The course is intended for intermediate users of R who are
interested in community ecology, particularly in the areas of terrestrial
and wetland ecology, microbial ecology, and natural resource management.
You are strongly encouraged to use your own data sets (they should be clean
and already structured, see the document: ?recommendation if you
participate with your data?.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

-- 

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] online course on Adaptation Genomics
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
There are only 3 seats left for our upcoming online course on Adaptation Genomics, taking place from 24-28 June . This course offers a comprehensive introduction to the genomic basis of adaptation, focusing on population genomics approaches.
 
Course website :[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/courseadaptationgenomics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/courseadaptationgenomics/ )
 

Learning Outcomes

1.    Handling genomic data from raw reads to genetic variants
 2.    Calculating basic population genetic statistics
 3.    Visualizing genetic population structure
 4.    Detecting genomic regions linked to adaptive divergence or local adaptation
 5.    Detecting signatures of selection in the genome
 6.    Detecting putative structural variants of adaptive significance
 7.    Understanding the potential and the limitations of different methods to study the genomic basis of adaptation
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Conservation Genomics course (online)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
If you are intereested in Conservation Genomics, have a look at our online course in July: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ )
 
 In this course, we will teach basic bioinformatics skills and how to manipulate, visualize and interpret genomic data and patterns in a conservation related context.
 
Learning Outcomes: 
1. Study design & genomic data collection methods 
2. Handling genomic data from raw reads to SNP genotypes 
3. Assessing population structure 
4. Searching for signals of adaptation 
5. Estimating Ne 
6. Calculating inbreeding 
7. Estimating relatedness
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846




	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Online course: Conservation Genomics (July 22-25)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

there are only a few seats left for the upcoming online course on **Conservation Genomics** from July 22nd-25th. 
 
 
 

This course introduces how population genomics tools can inform conservation efforts. Participants will learn about study design, genomic data collection, handling raw genomic data, and SNP filtering to explore population structure, local adaptation, effective population size, inbreeding, and relatedness through hands-on exercises and theoretical background.


Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course62/ ) 
 
 
 

Learning Outcomes:
1. Study design and genomic data collection methods
2. Handling genomic data from raw reads to SNP genotypes
3. Assessing population structure
4. Searching for signals of adaptation
5. Estimating effective population size
6. Calculating inbreeding
7. Estimating relatedness

For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )
 


Best regards,

Carlo
 
 
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846
